matches.x: AUL, and. Silvanus, and Timotheus, servants of God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ, unto the church of the Thessalonians ; grace unto you, and peace from God our Father, and the Lord Jesus Christ. 2 We give thanks always, making mention of you all, in our prayers to God for you.

You shall have power . . . in the demonstration of my Holy Spirit. The gospel is a living thing, not simply a list of precepts. Although we speak of the Bible and the Book of Mormon as containing the fulness of the gospel, in the true and proper sense the gospel can be found only in the lives of those who live it. We would not attempt to argue that a particular society had laws simply because…

Macedonia and Achaia were the northern and southern provinces, respectively, of Ancient Greece.

19. And gave unto them commandments that they should love and serve
 Him, the only living and true God, and that He should be the only
 being whom they should worship.

When Paul began preaching in Thessalonica, he taught for three straight Sabbaths in the synagogue (Acts 17:2-3). Those in attendance included Jews who had previously been scattered into Gentile lands and those native Macedonians who had been raised to believe in all of the Greek Gods. These Greeks were interested, for whatever reason, in the Jewish religious tradition and also attended the…
